blind_spots.ciprofloxacin: PMQR genes give low-level resistance; AcrAB-TolC overexpression beyond the curated regulator points (e.g. novel marR alleles) is invisible; point-mutation calls only fire with --organism Escherichia and only for curated substitutions (absence of a POINT row != wild type — feeds the callability gate).
blind_spots.gentamicin: 16S rRNA target mutations (e.g. rrs 1406/1408) are not in AMRFinderPlus's E. coli curated point set (they are in PointFinder only); novel AME alleles below BLAST identity cutoffs; porin/ uptake defects are not genomically visible.
blind_spots.ampicillin: ftsI (PBP3) substitutions raise ampicillin MICs modestly and are not counted here; porin loss alone rarely crosses the ampicillin breakpoint. Coverage of the acquired-enzyme mechanism is effectively complete.
blind_spots.trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole: folA (DHFR target of trimethoprim) promoter-up / coding mutations are in NEITHER the PointFinder E. coli panel NOR AMRFinderPlus's Escherichia point set — a real trimethoprim blind spot; efflux-mediated co-resistance not visible.
blind_spots.cefotaxime: ampC hyperexpression via novel promoter mutations or insertion sequences not in the curated point set; ESBL + porin-loss combinations (porin side invisible unless a curated omp point fires); novel ESBL alleles called only at family level (Subclass may fall back to BETA-LACTAM when phenotype is uncurated, amrfp_interpreting).
